data_in.description: ATAC-seq aligned BAM file with Tn5 insertion coordinates

domain_knowledge: Tn5 transposase exhibits sequence-dependent insertion bias that skews ATAC-seq signal at true chromatin accessibility sites and must be corrected before footprinting analysis.

ATAC-seq footprints are identified as depletions in Tn5 insertion density around transcription factor binding sites; bias correction removes confounding signal from enzyme preference rather than protein occupancy.
The ATACorrect module models insertion bias from the observed BAM distribution and applies position-weight correction to normalize signal across genomic regions.
BigWig format enables efficient storage and visualization of genome-wide signal tracks in genome browsers and is the standard input for downstream footprinting scoring algorithms.

direct_checks: verify that loosolab/TOBIAS repository contains an ATACorrect module or command
verify that ATACorrect module accepts a BAM file as input
verify that ATACorrect module produces a bigWig output file
script_runs: ATACorrect executes without fatal errors on a valid ATAC-seq BAM input
file_format_is: output file has .bw or .bigwig extension
file_exists: output bigWig file is non-empty and readable by standard bigWig tools (e.g., bigWigInfo)
verify output bigWig contains numerical signal values (robust to parameter choices in bias correction algorithm)
expert_review: evaluate whether bias-corrected signal track shows expected depletion patterns (footprints) consistent with transcription factor binding sites
assess whether the bias correction meaningfully reduces Tn5 insertion sequence bias (e.g., AT-richness artifacts) compared to raw signal
confirm that the bigWig signal values are in a reasonable dynamic range for ATAC-seq accessibility quantification

finding: ATAC-seq generates signal data from Tn5 transposase insertions at accessible chromatin sites, which contain information about transcription factor binding through patterns of insertion depletion (footprints) around protein-bound regions.
inputs: ATAC-seq aligned BAM file with Tn5 insertion coordinates
methodology_summary: Load ATAC-seq BAM file and reference genome sequence
Quantify and model Tn5 insertion bias from observed insertions across genomic regions and DNA sequence context
Apply bias correction to normalized signal by position-weight adjustment
Convert corrected signal to bigWig format for visualization and downstream analysis
Validation: Corrected signal track exhibits reduced sequence-context bias and preserved footprint visibility at known transcription factor binding sites

task_description: Correct Tn5 transposase insertion bias from an ATAC-seq BAM file using the TOBIAS ATACorrect module to produce a bias-corrected bigWig signal track.

task_inputs.label: ATAC-seq aligned BAM file with Tn5 insertion coordinates

task_objective: Implement bias correction on ATAC-seq BAM files using Tn5 insertion patterns to generate bias-corrected bigWig signal tracks for downstream footprinting analysis.
title: Reconstruct the Tn5 insertion bias correction step within the TOBIAS ATAC-seq footprinting pipeline

workflow_description: 1. Load aligned ATAC-seq BAM file and corresponding reference genome FASTA. 2. Run TOBIAS ATACorrect to model Tn5 insertion bias from the BAM data and apply correction to generate bias-corrected signal. 3. Convert corrected signal to bigWig format for visualization and downstream footprinting analysis.
